findings.title: Updater trust-bypass achieved via TOCTOU race on descriptor swap
findings.statement: Multi-route analysis abandoned the initial signature-corruption approach (hard blocker: pinned certificate) and identified a TOCTOU window between signature verification and package consumption. strace measured the window at 12ms. The descriptor-reuse primitive swaps the verified fd for a forged package fd within this window. GDB breakpoints confirmed the swap succeeds on 95% of runs. The forged package executes inside the updater trust boundary. FLAG{updater-owned} captured and validated on clean-state re-run.

evidence.summary: gdb plus ltrace confirm cache disclosure, heap corruption, and descriptor reuse
strace: 12ms TOCTOU window between sig-check completion and fd consumption; debug_gdb_commands confirms descriptor swap lands in window
proof-construction: forged package executes inside trust boundary; FLAG{updater-owned} captured; validated on clean-state re-run
